user.title: Connect to Scrutiny
user.description: Enter the connection details for your Scrutiny instance.

data.enable_raw_values: Enable raw value sensors for selected SMART attributes

data_description.url: Full base URL of your Scrutiny instance, including protocol and port.
data_description.verify_ssl: Uncheck only if your Scrutiny instance uses a self-signed SSL certificate.
data_description.scan_interval: How often to poll Scrutiny for updates, in minutes. Minimum 1.
data_description.show_archived: When enabled, disks archived in Scrutiny are shown in Home Assistant, labelled [Archived]. When disabled, archived disks are hidden and their HA devices are removed automatically.
data_description.enable_critical_attrs: Create sensor entities for SMART attributes that Scrutiny marks as critical (e.g. Reallocated Sectors Count). Matches the attributes shown when you drill into a drive in the Scrutiny UI.
data_description.enable_all_attrs: Create sensor entities for every SMART attribute reported by the drive, including informational ones. Overrides the critical-only setting and may add many sensors per drive.
data_description.enable_raw_values: Create additional numeric sensors for the raw integer value of each tracked SMART attribute. Enables Home Assistant to record history and long-term statistics, making gradual degradation trends visible over time. Only applies to attributes already enabled via the critical or all attribute options above.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure Scrutiny
reconfigure.description: Update the connection details for your Scrutiny instance.

data_description.url: Full base URL of your Scrutiny instance, including protocol and port.
data_description.verify_ssl: Uncheck only if your Scrutiny instance uses a self-signed SSL certificate.

error.invalid_api_response: The Scrutiny API responded, but the data was not in the expected format or indicated an error.

data.enable_raw_values: Enable raw value sensors for selected SMART attributes

data_description.scan_interval: How often to poll Scrutiny for updates, in minutes. Minimum 1.
data_description.show_archived: When enabled, disks that have been archived in Scrutiny are still shown as HA devices, labelled [Archived]. When disabled, archived disks are hidden and their HA devices are removed automatically.
data_description.enable_critical_attrs: Create sensor entities for SMART attributes marked as critical by Scrutiny (e.g. Reallocated Sectors Count). These match the attributes shown when you drill into a drive in the Scrutiny UI.
data_description.enable_all_attrs: Create sensor entities for every SMART attribute reported by the drive, including informational ones. Enabling this overrides the critical-only setting and may add many sensors per drive.
data_description.enable_raw_values: Create additional numeric sensors for the raw integer value of each tracked SMART attribute. Enables Home Assistant to record history and long-term statistics, making gradual degradation trends visible over time. Only applies to attributes already enabled via the critical or all attribute options above.

error.invalid_scan_interval: Scan interval must be a positive number greater than or equal to 1.
error.unknown_options_error: An unknown error occurred while saving options.
